text: Old Testament

Studies that explore the books written before the coming of Christ — from the Law and the Prophets to the Writings. The Old Testament tells the story of God's covenant with Israel and points forward to the Messiah.
Law
The first five books of the Bible (the Pentateuch), covering creation, the fall, the exodus, and the giving of the Law — the foundation on which the rest of Scripture builds.
Genesis
The book of beginnings: creation, the fall, and the patriarchs — Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and Joseph.
Exodus
God rescues his people from slavery in Egypt, reveals his name, and gives his Law at Sinai.
Leviticus
Instructions for worship and holiness, showing how a sinful people can draw near to a holy God.
Numbers
Israel's wilderness journey — its failures, its discipline, and God's continued faithfulness.
Deuteronomy
Moses' farewell sermons, renewing the covenant and preparing Israel to enter the Promised Land.
History
The historical books trace Israel's story from the conquest of Canaan through the monarchy, exile, and return — showing God's faithfulness and his people's repeated unfaithfulness.
Joshua
The conquest and division of the Promised Land under Joshua's leadership.
Judges
A cycle of rebellion, oppression, and rescue as Israel repeatedly turns from God.
Ruth
A story of loyalty, providence, and redemption set in the time of the judges.
1 Samuel
The transition from judges to kings: Samuel, Saul, and the rise of David.
2 Samuel
David's reign — his triumphs, failures, and God's covenant with his house.
1 Kings
Solomon's glory and the kingdom's division; Elijah and the contest on Mount Carmel.
2 Kings
The decline and fall of both kingdoms, ending in exile.
1 Chronicles
A retelling of Israel's history from Adam to David, focused on worship and the temple.
2 Chronicles
Solomon and the temple, the divided kingdom, and the hope of restoration.
Ezra
The return from exile and the rebuilding of the temple under Ezra's leadership.
Nehemiah
The rebuilding of Jerusalem's walls and the renewal of the covenant community.
Esther
God's hidden providence protecting his people in the Persian court.
Poetry & Wisdom
Job, Psalms, Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, and Song of Songs — books that explore the depths of human experience: suffering, praise, wisdom, and love.
Job
A profound exploration of suffering, justice, and the mystery of God's ways.
Psalms
Israel's songbook and prayer book — the full range of human emotion brought before God.
Proverbs
Practical wisdom for everyday life, rooted in the fear of the Lord.
Ecclesiastes
A searching examination of meaning, work, and mortality 'under the sun'.
Song of Songs
A celebration of love and marriage, rich with imagery of delight and longing.
Prophets

The prophetic books call Israel to repentance, warn of judgement, and point forward to God's ultimate redemption. They are essential for understanding the New Testament's fulfilment of those promises.
Isaiah
Judgement and breathtaking hope — including the Servant Songs that point to Christ.
Jeremiah
A prophet's anguish as Jerusalem falls, and the promise of a new covenant.
Lamentations
Raw grief over the destruction of Jerusalem, held together by hope in God's faithfulness.
Ezekiel
Visions of God's glory departing and returning, and the promise of spiritual renewal.
Daniel
Faithful presence in exile, and visions of God's sovereign kingdom over all nations.
Hosea
God's unfailing love for an unfaithful people, pictured through Hosea's own marriage.
Joel
A locust plague as a call to repentance, and the promise of the Spirit.
Amos
A sharp prophetic call for justice and the warning that empty religion will not stand.
Obadiah
A brief oracle against Edom and the coming day of the Lord.
Jonah
God's compassion for the nations — and a reluctant prophet's resistance to it.
Micah
Justice, mercy, and humility — and the promise of a ruler from Bethlehem.
Nahum
The fall of Nineveh and the comfort of God's justice for his oppressed people.
Habakkuk
Wrestling honestly with God over injustice, and learning to trust in his timing.
Zephaniah
The day of the Lord and the joy of restoration for a purified remnant.
Haggai
A call to rebuild the temple and prioritise God's house over personal comfort.
Zechariah
Visions of restoration and a Messiah who comes humbly on a donkey.
Malachi
A final call to faithfulness before the long silence before John the Baptist.
New Testament

Studies in the books written after the coming of Christ — the Gospels, the letters of the apostles, and Revelation. The New Testament reveals the fulfilment of God's promises in Jesus and the life of the early church.
Gospels & Acts
The four accounts of Jesus's life, death, and resurrection, and the story of the early church's expansion across the Roman world.
Matthew
Jesus as the fulfilment of the Old Testament, the new Moses, and the King of God's kingdom.
Mark
A fast-paced account of Jesus as the suffering Servant and Son of God.
Luke
Jesus as Saviour of the world — with special attention to the poor, women, and outsiders.
John
The deepest theological portrait of Jesus — the Word made flesh, the I AM, the Son of God.
Acts
The Holy Spirit empowers the church to take the gospel from Jerusalem to the ends of the earth.
Paul's Letters
Paul's letters to churches and individuals — exploring the logic of the gospel, Christian conduct, and the nature of the church.
Romans
Paul's most systematic exposition of the gospel: sin, justification, sanctification, and hope.
1 Corinthians
Addressing divisions and disorders in Corinth, with teaching on love, gifts, and the resurrection.
2 Corinthians
Paul's defence of his ministry and the paradox of strength through weakness.
Galatians
A passionate defence of justification by faith alone against those adding the law.
Ephesians
The riches of the believer's identity in Christ, and the church as God's new humanity.
Philippians
Joy in all circumstances and the example of Christ's humility.
Colossians
Christ as supreme over all things, and the implications for everyday life.
1 Thessalonians
Encouragement for a young church, with teaching on the return of Christ.
2 Thessalonians
Correction of misunderstandings about the end times, and a call to faithful work.
1 Timothy
Instructions for church life, leadership, and sound doctrine.
2 Timothy
Paul's final letter — a charge to Timothy to guard the gospel and finish well.
Titus
Appointing leaders and teaching that sound doctrine leads to godly living.
Philemon
A personal appeal for reconciliation and the social implications of the gospel.
General Letters & Revelation
Letters from James, Peter, John, Jude, and others — covering faith in trials, genuine belief, false teaching, and the ultimate hope of Revelation.
Hebrews
Jesus as the fulfilment of all the Old Testament's priesthood, sacrifice, and covenant — superior in every way.
James
Faith that works — practical wisdom for living as God's people in the world.
1 Peter
Hope and holy living for exiles in a hostile world.
2 Peter
Warnings against false teachers and confidence in God's promises.
1 John
Assurance of salvation and the marks of genuine faith: love, obedience, and truth.
2 John
A brief letter on love, truth, and not welcoming those who deny Christ.
3 John
Commending faithful hospitality and warning against self-seeking leadership.
Jude
A strong call to contend for the faith against false teaching that has crept into the church.
Revelation
Visions of God's ultimate victory over evil, the judgement of the wicked, and the renewal of all things.
Beliefs
Studies focused on Christian doctrine — what the Bible teaches about God, humanity, salvation, and the future. Grounding ourselves in right belief is the foundation for right living.
Overview
Introductory studies surveying the whole of Christian teaching — the gospel, the core doctrines, and the historic confessions of the church.
Foundations
The essential building blocks of Christian belief — what every believer needs to know.
The Gospel
Studies focused on the heart of the Christian message: sin, grace, and salvation through Christ.
Creeds & Confessions
The historic statements of faith that have defined orthodox Christianity across the centuries.
God
Who is God? Studies exploring the Trinity, God's attributes, and what it means to know him personally.
Attributes of God
God's holiness, love, sovereignty, justice, and the other characteristics Scripture reveals.
The Trinity
One God in three persons — Father, Son, and Holy Spirit — and why this matters.
God the Father
God as Creator, King, and Father — and what it means to be his child.
Scripture
Studies on the Bible itself — how it came to be, what it claims about itself, and how to read it well.
Authority
Why the Bible has the right to speak into every area of life.
Inspiration
How God worked through human authors to give us his Word.
Canon
How the books of the Bible were recognised and gathered together.
Interpretation
Principles for reading the Bible faithfully and accurately.
Creation & Humanity
Studies on what it means to be human — made in God's image, embodied, and created for a purpose.
Creation
God as Creator of all things — and what that means for how we view the world.
Image of God
What it means to be made in God's likeness, and the dignity that gives every person.
Embodiment
The goodness of having a body, and what Scripture says about physical existence.
Purpose
Why we are here — the human calling to love God, serve others, and steward creation.
Evil and the Fall
Studies on sin — its origin, its nature, and its devastating effects on human beings and the world.
Nature of Sin
What sin actually is — not just rule-breaking, but a fundamental turning away from God.
The Fall
What happened in Genesis 3, and how it affects every human being.
Temptation
How temptation works, and how believers can stand firm against it.
Christ
Studies on the person of Jesus Christ — fully God and fully human — and what his coming means.
Person of Christ
The doctrine of the two natures — how Jesus is both fully divine and fully human.
Incarnation
Why the Son of God became flesh, and what his humanity means for us.
Salvation
How does God rescue sinners? Studies covering atonement, justification, adoption, and the whole sweep of what God does to save us.
Atonement
How Christ's death deals with sin — substitution, redemption, reconciliation, and more.
Conversion
What it means to turn from sin and trust in Christ — repentance and faith.
Justification
Being declared righteous before God — by grace alone, through faith alone, in Christ alone.
Adoption
The extraordinary gift of being brought into God's family as his sons and daughters.
The Spirit
Studies on the Holy Spirit — who he is, what he does, and how he works in believers and the church.
Person of the Spirit
The Holy Spirit as the third person of the Trinity — not a force, but a person.
Fruit of the Spirit
The character qualities the Spirit produces in those who walk with him.
Spiritual Gifts
The gifts the Spirit gives to equip believers for service in the body of Christ.
Church
What is the church, and why does it matter? Studies on the nature, mission, and structure of the body of Christ.
Nature of Church
What the church is — the people of God, the body of Christ, the temple of the Spirit.
Sacraments
Baptism and the Lord's Supper — what they are, what they mean, and how to observe them.
Leadership
Biblical patterns of church leadership: elders, deacons, and servant-hearted authority.
Worship
What Christian worship is, why it matters, and how it shapes the life of the community.
Last Things
Studies on death, resurrection, judgement, and the new creation — the hope that shapes how Christians live in the present.
Return of Christ
The certain hope that Jesus will return in glory to judge the living and the dead.
Judgment
God's final verdict on all people — and the good news that Christ has borne judgment for his own.
Heaven & Hell
The eternal destinies Scripture describes — and why they matter for how we live now.
Life
Studies that connect biblical truth to the everyday experience of following Christ — in relationships, work, identity, and the ongoing struggle against sin.
Spiritual Disciplines
Practices that train us to stay close to God and grow in godliness — not ways to earn favour, but habits that keep us open to his grace.
Prayer
Talking with God — its foundations, its forms, and how to develop a richer prayer life.
Bible Reading
How to read Scripture well — with understanding, consistency, and an expectation of being changed.
Worship
Worship as a whole-life posture, not just a Sunday activity.
Character & Sanctification
The ongoing process of becoming more like Christ — fighting sin, growing in virtue, and understanding who we are in him.
Fruit of the Spirit
Love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, and self-control.
Overcoming Sin
Practical and theological help for dealing with persistent patterns of sin.
Growth in Grace
What it looks like to grow as a Christian — and what gets in the way.
Identity in Christ
Who we are because of what Christ has done — and why that changes everything.
Relationships & Family
Studies on the relationships God has placed us in — marriage, family, friendship, and community.
Marriage
The covenant of marriage — its purpose, its challenges, and its picture of Christ and the church.
Singleness
Singleness as a full and meaningful calling, not a waiting room for marriage.
Parenting
Raising children in the faith — with grace, truth, and a long-term perspective.
Friendship
The gift of Christian friendship — and how to cultivate relationships that go deep.
Communication
Speaking truthfully and kindly — and listening well.
Community
What it means to live as the body of Christ — bearing one another's burdens.
Work & Vocation
Studies applying the gospel to work, money, time, and ambition — seeing everyday labour as part of God's purposes.
Work Life
What the Bible says about work — its goodness, its frustrations, and its redemption in Christ.
Money & Possessions
Generosity, contentment, and a biblical view of wealth.
Stewardship of Time
How we use our time — and how the gospel reshapes our priorities.
Ambition
Holy ambition vs. selfish ambition — and how to tell the difference.
Retirement
What faithful living looks like in the later seasons of life.
Christian Ethics & Culture
How believers think and act on the pressing ethical questions of our time — from social justice to bioethics to technology.
Social Justice
God's heart for the vulnerable and the oppressed, and what that means for us.
Bioethics
Beginning and end of life issues — approached with theological care and compassion.
Engaging Technology
How to use technology faithfully in a world that is shaped by it.
Suffering & Guidance
Studies that help believers walk through pain, uncertainty, and difficulty with honesty and hope.
Trials and Affliction
Why God allows suffering, and how Scripture equips us to endure it with hope.
Discerning God's Will
How to make wise decisions — and what Scripture actually promises about guidance.
Mental Health
Depression, anxiety, and other struggles — approached with compassion and biblical care.
Grief
How to grieve as those who have hope — honestly, and not alone.
Ministry
Studies for those involved in serving others — in evangelism, pastoral care, teaching, and leadership. Rooted in Scripture, these studies equip believers to serve effectively and sustainably.
Theology of Ministry
The biblical foundations for all Christian ministry — what it means to serve, and what kind of person the servant must be.
Servant Leadership
Leading as Jesus led — with humility, sacrifice, and care for those being served.
Calling
Understanding your calling to ministry — how it is discerned and what it involves.
Preaching & Teaching (Homiletics)
Studies for those called to explain the Bible publicly — covering method, character, and the weight of handling God's Word.
Expository Preaching
How to preach faithfully from the text — letting the Bible set the agenda.
Leading Bible Studies
How to lead a group well — asking good questions and helping people meet God in the text.
Leadership & Administration
The practical and theological dimensions of leading a church or ministry — vision, teams, conflict, and governance.
Vision & Strategy
How to think clearly about where God is leading and how to get there together.
Team Building
Gathering, developing, and sustaining a healthy ministry team.
Conflict Resolution
How to handle disagreement and division in a way that honours God and preserves unity.
Church Governance
The structures and processes that help a church be accountable and well-ordered.
Pastoral Care
Studies equipping believers to walk alongside one another in difficulty, loss, crisis, and need.
Pastoral Conversations
The skills and posture needed for helpful one-to-one ministry conversations.
Crisis Intervention
How to respond wisely and compassionately when someone is in acute distress.
Visitation
The ministry of presence — visiting the sick, the elderly, and the housebound.
Marriage Prep
Helping couples prepare for a marriage built on Christ.
Bereavement Care
Walking with those who are grieving — what to say, what not to say, and how to stay.
Evangelism & Missions
Studies on taking the gospel to those who haven't heard it — personally, locally, and across cultures.
Personal Witnessing
How to share your faith naturally and clearly in everyday conversation.
Apologetics
Answering the questions that keep people from taking Christianity seriously.
Cross-Cultural Ministry
Serving and sharing across cultural boundaries with sensitivity and respect.
Church Planting
The theology and practice of establishing new gospel communities.
Discipleship & Mentoring
Studies on the ministry of helping others grow — one to one, in small groups, and by training the next generation.
One-to-One Ministry
The power of intentional, personal discipleship — meeting regularly to open the Bible together.
Small Group Leading
How to lead a small group that is genuine, growing, and grounded in Scripture.
Training Others
Equipping others to do the work of ministry — multiplying not just converts but leaders.
Other
Studies that don't fit neatly into another category — or whose shelf information hasn't been set yet.
